title: delphinium carolinianum subsp. penardii
intro: Delphinium carolinianum subsp. penardii, also known as the mountain delphinium, is a subspecies of perennial plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Delphinium carolinianum subsp. penardii has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and acting as an antispasmodic agent. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
